mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high RSAFS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise but SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that boosts corporate profit margins through wage pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction with other proxies is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, which reduces corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines but SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent job gains (PAYEMS) above 150k per month indicate a robust labor market that increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS exceeds 150k but SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction with other labor indicators is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal stagflationary pressures that reduce equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines and CPIAUCSL rises but SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction effect size < 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.
